perturbation.original_text: (1) "Configuration" means the preparation of a computer and related ------------- hardware and integration of components into a computer system; provided -------- that the term "Configuration" shall not include installation of a computer or related hardware at a customer site.
perturbation.changed_text: (1) "Configuration" means the preparation of a computer and related ------------- hardware and integration of components into a computer system; provided -------- that the term "Configuration" shall include installation of a computer or related hardware at a customer site.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of "Configuration" to include installation at the customer site, we've introduced a contradiction with section 3.1(c) and the definition of Product Business. The original definition specifically excluded installation, whereas the modified definition includes it. This affects the Non-Competition Agreement by creating an ambiguity as to whether Seller is allowed to do installations at customer sites, as section 3.1(c) states that seller agrees that during the Non-Competition Period, it will not actively encourage any Services customer to move its initial image load/configuration business from CompuCom's configuration facilities to the customer`s site.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Seller agrees that during the Non-Competition Period Seller will, and will cause its Subsidiaries to use commercially reasonable efforts to cause future Service customers to whom Seller provides Outsourcing and Professional Services and existing Service customers of Seller to whom Seller is able to expand its Services, to procure Products and image load/configuration from CompuCom in accordance with the provisions of Article IV, provided that CompuCom is not competing with Seller to provide future or expanded Services to such customers. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Seller shall be permitted to preserve its ability to resell Products to the extent required by existing customer agreements if (i) the customer refuses to approve of CompuCom providing such Products after Seller uses commercially reasonable efforts to obtain such approval, (ii) CompuCom rejects the customer order for such Products pursuant to Article IV (provided that CompuCom shall comply with the requirements of the Asset Purchase Agreement with respect to such agreements), or (iii) CompuCom fails to meet the requirements of the customer agreement, including but not limited to price, payment terms, return privileges and service level agreements ("SLA's) (subject to applicable cure periods). In addition, Seller shall be permitted (A) to arrange for the procurement of computers or related hardware and software products as agent for customers in connection with future Outsourcing and Professional Services engagements, subject to Seller's obligations under Section 3.1(b) and the conditions provided for in the immediately preceeding sentence (B) to refer orders for Products for delivery outside the
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly excludes 'installation of a computer or related hardware at a customer site' from the definition of 'Configuration'. The changed text includes it. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, as the explanation states it affects the Non-Competition Agreement by creating an ambiguity as to whether Seller is allowed to do installations at customer sites, especially in light of section 3.1(c), which the explanation refers to. This is a strong contradiction because the change directly alters a definition, making a previously prohibited activity potentially permissible, thereby conflicting with the intentions of the Non-Competition Agreement and section 3.1(c).

perturbation.original_text: (7) "Services" means all IT services offered by Seller, including, -------- but not limited to, all outsourcing, professional services, break/fix, staff augmentation and consulting services; provided that the term -------- "Services" shall not include (i) on-site Configuration of Products by CompuCom or (ii) the sale (but not the performance) by CompuCom of extended warranty contracts at time of initial sale of Products to customers.
perturbation.changed_text: (7) "Services" means all IT services offered by Seller, including, -------- but not limited to, all outsourcing, professional services, break/fix, staff augmentation and consulting services; provided that the term -------- "Services" shall not include (ii) the sale (but not the performance) by CompuCom of extended warranty contracts at time of initial sale of Products to customers.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the exclusion of "(i) on-site Configuration of Products by CompuCom", from the definition of 'Services', we introduce a contradiction in Article III, Section 3.2, which states CompuCom will not provide any services to accounts listed on Exhibit B and C. Now, since onsite configuration could be considered a service, It becomes uncertain whether CompuCom is allowed to do so for those accounts, due to the in-text definition contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 CompuCom's Agreement. (a) CompuCom agrees that during the Non- -------------------- Competition Period CompuCom will not, and will not permit any of its Subsidiaries to: (i) provide any Services to any account listed on Exhibit B hereto; -------- (ii) provide any Services to any account listed on Exhibit C hereto except to the extent provided for in such Exhibit; or (iii) except as contemplated by the Asset Purchase Agreement, solicit, entice or induce any employee of Seller or any Subsidiary of Seller to terminate his or her employment with Seller or any Subsidiary of Seller or hire any person who was or is at any time from the date of execution of the Asset Purchase Agreement to the end of the Non-Competition Period an employee of Seller or any Subsidiary of Seller.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the exclusion of "(i) on-site Configuration of Products by CompuCom" from the definition of 'Services'. The contradicted text (Section 3.2) states that CompuCom will not provide any Services to accounts listed on Exhibits B and C. Since onsite configuration could now be considered a service due to the removal of its exclusion in the definition, this directly contradicts Section 3.2, creating uncertainty as to whether CompuCom is allowed to perform onsite configuration for accounts on Exhibits B and C. The change alters the definition of services, which directly impacts the obligations outlined in Section 3.2, fitting the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: ARTICLE IV Acceptance of Product Orders CompuCom agrees that during the Non-Competition Period, it will accept orders for Products submitted to CompuCom by Seller pursuant to Section 3.1(b) under the following conditions: (i) CompuCom has the ability to offer such Product for sale; (ii) the purchase price required by the customer order for the Product is reasonably acceptable to CompuCom; (iii) such Product offered by CompuCom meets the customer's specifications, including, but not limited to, configuration specifications; and (iv) the requirements of the customer with respect to such Product, including but not limited to delivery, payment terms and return privileges are reasonably acceptable to CompuCom. CompuCom agrees to notify Seller of CompuCom's rejection of such orders for Product within a mutually agreed upon time that is reasonable under the time constraints required by the customer order or contract.
perturbation.changed_text: ARTICLE IV Acceptance of Product Orders CompuCom agrees that during the Non-Competition Period, it will accept orders for Products submitted to CompuCom by Seller pursuant to Section 3.1(b) under the following conditions: (i) CompuCom has the ability to offer such Product for sale; (ii) the purchase price required by the customer order for the Product is reasonably acceptable to CompuCom; (iii) such Product offered by CompuCom meets the customer's specifications, including, but not limited to, configuration specifications; and (iv) the requirements of the customer with respect to such Product, including but not limited to delivery, payment terms and return privileges are reasonably acceptable to CompuCom.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence, "CompuCom agrees to notify Seller of CompuCom's rejection of such orders for Product within a mutually agreed upon time that is reasonable under the time constraints required by the customer order or contract.", it creates a contradiction in Article III, Section 3.1 (b), because that section requires CompuCom to not reject seller orders pursuant to Article IV. Without a time limit requirement on CompuCom's rejection, then CompuCom can reject orders with no time limit creating an in-text conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Seller agrees that during the Non-Competition Period Seller will, and will cause its Subsidiaries to use commercially reasonable efforts to cause future Service customers to whom Seller provides Outsourcing and Professional Services and existing Service customers of Seller to whom Seller is able to expand its Services, to procure Products and image load/configuration from CompuCom in accordance with the provisions of Article IV, provided that CompuCom is not competing with Seller to provide future or expanded Services to such customers. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Seller shall be permitted to preserve its ability to resell Products to the extent required by existing customer agreements if (i) the customer refuses to approve of CompuCom providing such Products after Seller uses commercially reasonable efforts to obtain such approval, (ii) CompuCom rejects the customer order for such Products pursuant to Article IV (provided that CompuCom shall comply with the requirements of the Asset Purchase Agreement with respect to such agreements), or (iii) CompuCom fails to meet the requirements of the customer agreement, including but not limited to price, payment terms, return privileges and service level agreements ("SLA's) (subject to applicable cure periods). In addition, Seller shall be permitted (A) to arrange for the procurement of computers or related hardware and software products as agent for customers in connection with future Outsourcing and Professional Services engagements, subject to Seller's obligations under Section 3.1(b) and the conditions provided for in the immediately preceeding sentence (B) to refer orders for Products for delivery outside the
perturbation.justification: The original text contained a requirement for CompuCom to notify the seller of rejection of orders within a mutually agreed upon time. The changed text removes this notification requirement. The contradicted text, Section 3.1(b), states that CompuCom can reject seller orders pursuant to Article IV. However, without the notification requirement of the original text, CompuCom could effectively reject orders without any time constraints, which creates a contradiction because it diminishes the seller's ability to rely on Article IV as written in the original contract, which required notification of rejection in a reasonable timeframe. This is a strong contradiction as it affects the seller's ability to fulfill its obligations under Section 3.1(b).

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 Seller's Agreement. (a) Seller agrees that during the Non- ------------------ Competition Period Seller will not, and will not permit any of its Subsidiaries to: (i) engage in the Product Business; (ii) encourage any Service Account to specify a party other than CompuCom to provide Products and image load/configurations to such Service Account; or (iii) solicit, entice or induce any employee of CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom to terminate his or her employment with CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom or hire any person who was or is at any time from the date of execution of the Asset Purchase Agreement to the end of the Non-Competition Period an employee of CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 Seller's Agreement. (a) Seller agrees that during the Non- ------------------ Competition Period Seller will not, and will not permit any of its Subsidiaries to: (ii) encourage any Service Account to specify a party other than CompuCom to provide Products and image load/configurations to such Service Account; or (iii) solicit, entice or induce any employee of CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom to terminate his or her employment with CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom or hire any person who was or is at any time from the date of execution of the Asset Purchase Agreement to the end of the Non-Competition Period an employee of CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "(i) engage in the Product Business;", it creates a contradiction in Section 3.1 (b) where Seller agrees to use commercially reasonable efforts to cause future Service customers to procure Products and image load/configuration from CompuCom. If Seller is allowed to engage in the Product Business, it is unclear if they are required to send their service customers to CompuCom, creating a contradiction between the two sections.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Seller agrees that during the Non-Competition Period Seller will, and will cause its Subsidiaries to use commercially reasonable efforts to cause future Service customers to whom Seller provides Outsourcing and Professional Services and existing Service customers of Seller to whom Seller is able to expand its Services, to procure Products and image load/configuration from CompuCom in accordance with the provisions of Article IV, provided that CompuCom is not competing with Seller to provide future or expanded Services to such customers. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Seller shall be permitted to preserve its ability to resell Products to the extent required by existing customer agreements if (i) the customer refuses to approve of CompuCom providing such Products after Seller uses commercially reasonable efforts to obtain such approval, (ii) CompuCom rejects the customer order for such Products pursuant to Article IV (provided that CompuCom shall comply with the requirements of the Asset Purchase Agreement with respect to such agreements), or (iii) CompuCom fails to meet the requirements of the customer agreement, including but not limited to price, payment terms, return privileges and service level agreements ("SLA's) (subject to applicable cure periods). In addition, Seller shall be permitted (A) to arrange for the procurement of computers or related hardware and software products as agent for customers in connection with future Outsourcing and Professional Services engagements, subject to Seller's obligations under Section 3.1(b) and the conditions provided for in the immediately preceeding sentence (B) to refer orders for Products for delivery outside the
perturbation.justification: The removal of section (i) "engage in the Product Business" from Section 3.1(a) directly contradicts Section 3.1(b). Section 3.1(b) requires the Seller to use commercially reasonable efforts to cause future Service customers to procure Products from CompuCom. If the Seller is now allowed to engage in the Product Business as a result of the omission in 3.1(a), this creates a conflict because it is unclear if the Seller is required to direct its Service customers to CompuCom or whether they can provide the Products themselves. Therefore, the omission leads to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to the limitations set forth in Article III, Seller and CompuCom agree that with respect to each account listed on Exhibit A hereto, --------- that until the earlier of (a) May 11, 2000 or (b) the termination of the existing contract between Seller and such account (i) each party will cooperate with the other in delivering Services and Products to such account in substantially the same manner in which such Services and Products were delivered to such -2- <PAGE> account prior to Closing and (ii) each party's representative will be permitted to call on such account.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the limitations set forth in Article III, Seller and CompuCom agree that with respect to each account listed on Exhibit A hereto, --------- that until the earlier of (a) May 11, 2000 or (b) the termination of the existing contract between Seller and such account (i) each party will cooperate with the other in delivering Services and Products to such account in substantially the same manner in which such Services and Products were delivered to such -2- <PAGE> account prior to Closing.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause "(ii) each party's representative will be permitted to call on such account.", it creates a contradiction with the clause "each party will cooperate with the other in delivering Services and Products to such account". Part of cooperating would involve each party contacting accounts and clients. By removing this clause it creates ambiguity on whether each party is cooperating or not, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the limitations set forth in Article III, Seller and CompuCom agree that with respect to each account listed on Exhibit A hereto, --------- that until the earlier of (a) May 11, 2000 or (b) the termination of the existing contract between Seller and such account (i) each party will cooperate with the other in delivering Services and Products to such account in substantially the same manner in which such Services and Products were delivered to such -2- <PAGE> account prior to Closing.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed each party's representative to call on accounts, supporting cooperation in delivering services. The changed text removes this ability, creating a contradiction because it now creates ambiguity on whether each party is cooperating or not. The contradicted text, which is the same as the original, assumes this right exists for cooperation, creating an in-text contradiction as defined by the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition provided.
